title: Document stage: per-ticket change doc for the merge-gate human
description: Add a fifth, post-gate 'document' stage so the merge-gate human reviews an organized technical delta (endpoints, schemas, components, pages, CLI surfaces) instead of raw diff + run logs. It runs exactly once per ticket, after the review stage passes the dual gate (outcome=success AND exit_signal=true) and before the workflow reports done - never inside the bounded plan->implement->test->review loop, and it can never send the ticket back to plan. The stage reads the ticket, git diff main...HEAD, and the run's prior stage outputs, then writes and commits docs/changes/<ticket-id>.md (kept forever; one file per ticket; docs/ is deliberately not a harness directory). Registration: add 'document' to STAGES in adw/state.py (adw/status.py validates stage names against it) and to STAGE_TOOLS in adw/invoke.py with scope Read, Glob, Grep, Write, the existing _GIT_READONLY rules, plus Bash(git add:*) and Bash(git commit:*); add "document": "sonnet" to configs/models.json. Prompt assets: commands/DOCUMENT.md (entry prompt; must state that the agent runs headless - nobody will answer questions, blockers go in the status block - and that the doc must be committed before stopping, since the Stop checklist's clean-tree rule applies to this writing stage) and stage_specs/document_feat.md (the contract). Doc template sections, each included only when that change-kind exists in the diff: What shipped; Surface changes with subsections Endpoints/APIs, Schemas & data, Components/pages, CLI/workflows; Behavior & breaking changes; How it was verified (acceptance criteria mapped to test evidence); Review notes; File map (changed files, one line of why each). Contract rules: every claim anchored in the actual diff (never the plan's intentions), organized delta not a diff dump, bounded length, no future-work speculation, README/architecture drift is noted in the doc as a suggested follow-up and never edited. Orchestrator: in adw/orchestrator.py run_ticket, after the dual gate passes, invoke the document stage once with exactly one retry on failure/timeout; persistent failure still returns done with a warning recorded on the TicketOutcome, and a breaker halt reason from the document stage downgrades to a warning rather than converting done into halted. No hook changes are needed.
acceptance_criteria: 'document' is registered in STAGES (adw/state.py) and STAGE_TOOLS (adw/invoke.py) with exactly Read, Glob, Grep, Write, the read-only git rules, Bash(git add:*), Bash(git commit:*); configs/models.json maps document to sonnet

commands/DOCUMENT.md and stage_specs/document_feat.md exist with the repo's standard front-matter (name, description, read_when, sdlc_stage), define the conditional section template (What shipped; Endpoints/APIs; Schemas & data; Components/pages; CLI/workflows; Behavior & breaking changes; How it was verified; Review notes; File map) and the anchored-in-diff / no-diff-dump / bounded-length / no-speculation rules; DOCUMENT.md states the headless rule and the commit-before-stop requirement
adw/orchestrator.py run_ticket invokes the document stage exactly once, only after review reports outcome=success with exit_signal=true, and never on iterations that loop back to plan

Document-stage failure or timeout is retried exactly once; persistent failure still returns outcome 'done' with the warning recorded on TicketOutcome; a breaker halt reason raised during the document stage becomes a warning, never a halted outcome

Unit tests with a fake invoke_fn cover: document runs once after the dual gate; document is not invoked when review fails or loops; document failure plus one retry still yields done with the warning recorded
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green
